title: Outgoing user-notification / messaging backbone
description: Datum has no outbound messaging surface to communicate with users. Design: typed Notice record in gui-protocol (id/source/severity/category/title/body/actions/condition/timestamp), presented by the shell as three tiers — transient toasts (bottom-right, auto-expire), sticky condition-banners (KiCad-infobar style, auto-clear when condition resolves), and a persistent Notices log behind a status-bar bell. Publishers state facts; shell decides presentation. Never journaled (consumer/env state, not a design mutation). Headless surfaces render same notices as text/JSON. Optional chime on warning+ severity, globally disableable, redundant with icon/color/text (accessibility). Shared-by-construction backbone, not a one-off dialog. Weave into UI_LAYOUT_SYSTEM_CONTRACT or a decision record + PROGRESS/Frontier.

title: DWT: notify user KDE 'disable touchpad while typing' blocks Space-drag pan
description: KDE Wayland default 'Disable while typing' suppresses touchpad motion whenever a key (incl. held Space) is down, blocking the Space+left-drag pan gesture. First client of the notification backbone: condition-bound sticky banner. Startup probe parses ~/.config/kcminputrc [Libinput][vid][pid][dev] groups — key DisableWhileTyping is ABSENT at the blocking default (present only once user unchecks it), so absence=trap-live is a deterministic signal. Escalate severity if no external mouse is enumerated (touchpad is the only pan device). Action button launches 'systemsettings kcm_touchpad'. Banner auto-clears when probe sees the fix. GNOME sibling adapter later (gsettings org.gnome.desktop.peripherals.touchpad disable-while-typing).

title: Terminal input-focus authority: workspace hotkeys leak into PTY; cannot type into terminal
description: Root cause: the gui-app keyboard router (crates/gui-app/src/main.rs ~L503-896) has no focus authority — it gates every arm on ui.active_dock_tab (is the dock OPEN) as a proxy for focus, and never consults pane focus (decision 021 / status-bar 'focus Board'). Symptoms: (1) with terminal dock open+attached, terminal_accepts_raw_input()=true so the raw-PTY arm swallows all keys — 'f' (board fit) is written to the shell (activity span in:1B out:1B | F), workspace tool hotkeys s/b/v/m/x are guarded on active_dock_tab.is_none() so they can't fire; screenshot shows leaked DDFFFFFFFF at the prompt. (2) Deliberate typing lands in a hidden legacy line-editor buffer when detached. Fix: one keyboard-focus owner (Editor(pane)|Terminal|Overlay); opening dock never steals keys; click/Ctrl-focus to enter terminal, Esc/canvas to leave; rewire the \x1b[I/\x1b[O focus sequences from WINDOW focus (main.rs:328) to TERMINAL focus; visible focus (block vs hollow cursor). Also: hotkeys fire on Release while PTY consumes on Press — move hotkeys to Press once routing is fixed. Blocks trustworthy terminal-as-messaging-destination.

title: Terminal has two rival input models (raw PTY vs legacy line-editor)
description: The dock terminal has two input paths selected implicitly by attach state: raw PTY byte writer, and a legacy structured line-editor (append_dock_text -> ui.terminal.input with its own cursor/history/tab-complete, main.rs ~L559-790). Detached typing silently lands in the hidden line buffer instead of the shell. Collapse to one: attached PTY gets raw bytes; detached shows a read-only screen with an explicit reattach affordance. The line-editor is a pre-PTY relic (two-rival-implementations defect class). Same subsystem as terminal-focus-authority.

title: Reconcile GUI-message/diagnostics surface with doctrine (Command Console + console sink, NOT an Output tab)
description: DOCTRINE CORRECTION (supersedes the original 'app-owned Output dock tab' framing). The GUI design spec explicitly VACATED a standalone Output/supervision tab (the decision-013 meta-supervision misfire); CAM/export results are files-in-workdir + gerber/drill/paperspace viewers, not a lane. So there is NO Output tab. But the legitimate need behind this issue is real: GUI-action echoes, engine messages, and diagnostics (incl. Codex's pan-trace) need a home that is NOT the foreign-shell PTY screen ('GUI actions must never write to the terminal'). Doctrine's sanctioned homes: (1) the viewport-anchored COMMAND CONSOLE (the editor's typed verb line — the 'typed twin of the marking menu', DATUM_GUI_DESIGN_SPEC 'three surfaces'), which does not exist yet and is the real frontier item; (2) the existing model-only ConsoleLaneState sink (gui-protocol/workspace_layout.rs) where GUI-action narration lands until the console is built. This issue = build the Command Console surface + wire ConsoleLaneState to it. Belongs to the console/write-path track, NOT the terminal-emulator epic. Relates to the GUI write-path plan. See research/terminal-redesign/TERMINAL_CURRENT_STATE_MAP.md sec 5.

title: Migrate stray in-repo tracker files into beads
description: Two ad-hoc issue/backlog files predate this tracker and should fold in once beads is adopted: docs/gui/M7_IMPORTED_BOARD_FIDELITY_ISSUES.md (184 lines) and docs/DECOMPOSITION_BACKLOG.md (241 lines). Not bulk-migrated during the trial — item-by-item classification is owner/Codex judgment. Evidence that an unmanaged tracker need was already materializing in the repo.

title: Decide Application Status Bar retention and replacement
description: Owner decision intentionally deferred. Determine whether Datum removes the Application Status Bar or retains a minimal hideable global-health strip. Research found active-work fields are duplicated and belong near the engaged pane; revision and DRC/ERC/global progress need an explicit replacement map before removal. Compare the current prototype against a no-bar prototype with revision/project health in global project chrome, checks routed to canvas plus Checks surface, and layer counts in the Layers sidebar. Governing guidance: docs/gui/DATUM_APPLICATION_STATUS_BAR_GUIDANCE.md. Do not implement S5 status reporting or remove the strip until this decision closes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Same-size screenshots compare the controlling current prototype with a no-bar replacement prototype. 2. Every current field has an approved owner/location or explicit retirement. 3. DRC/ERC states distinguish clean, warning, error, running, and stale and open durable details. 4. Project revision remains visible without duplication. 5. Layer count moves to the Layers sidebar if useful. 6. Consequential feedback remains pane/canvas-local and accessible if global chrome is hidden. 7. Owner chooses remove or minimal retained strip. 8. Choice is ratified in the numbered GUI/selection decision, governed specs, prototype, Active Frontier, and conformance tests before implementation.
notes: Captured after research commit e409c9e. Current prototype already duplicates revision at top-right and bottom; focus/tool/selection/grid/layer have nearer owners. This issue is deliberately deferred so S5 selection specification can continue without forcing the shell decision.

title: Pan-gesture trace instrumentation pollutes the terminal screen
description: Live pan-gesture diagnostic instrumentation (append_gui_verbose_diagnostic_line, added by commit cf552bb 'chore(viewport): trace live pan gesture stages') renders ACTIVITY SPANS / 'selected terminal activity span' / pan-stage lines directly into the terminal dock screen, burying the shell prompt after keystrokes (visible in the 2026-07-11 screenshots where the prompt is swamped). This is temporary debugging output routed to the wrong sink — it corrupts a screen hosting a FOREIGN shell. Fix: remove the instrumentation once pan work stabilizes, or route it to a diagnostics sink (stderr / the future Output lane), never the PTY screen. Distinct from the focus-authority routing bug. Relates to output-lane (correct destination) and terminal-focus-authority (same terminal-screen surface).

title: Decide canonical 2D pan invocation (right-drag vs Space+drag both conflict)
description: The origin design tension of this session. 2D viewport pan needs an invocation gesture, but both candidates collide: (1) right-drag — the spec default — conflicts with the right-click context/marking menu in workspace panes; (2) Space+left-drag — the current remap (commits c604884/04e5465/da9e978) — is suppressed by KDE Wayland 'disable touchpad while typing', which kills touchpad motion while Space is held (see dat-dwt-touchpad-nag). Neither is clean on a trackpad. Need a ratified canonical pan-invocation design: research how pro CAD/EDA/DCC tools resolve pan-vs-context-menu-vs-modifier on trackpads (middle-drag, space-hold, dedicated tool, edge cases), decide Datum's model, and ratify it (numbered decision record + Active Frontier + conformance). Until then Space+drag ships with the dwt nag as mitigation, not resolution. Owner decision. Relates to dat-dwt-touchpad-nag.

title: Complete the S5 selection visual-language contract
description: Complete the remaining S5 selection visual-language contract. RESOLVED: cross-surface #CE5A92; slight coherent brightening of complete owned visible presentation while preserving semantic/material hues; internal soft glow plus crisp object-shaped 2-physical-pixel screen-space cue; whole-symbol/footprint ownership; selection-over-hover; screen-only/no retained/CAM mutation. REMAINING: full per-object PCB/schematic matrix; active/inactive workspace projection; compound/focus and locked/related/proposal/diagnostic precedence; global-net membership versus related styling; bus/text/point treatments; dense-selection budget/fallback; prototype review; and conformance gates. S5 cannot claim visual completeness until these close.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Rendering Book defines selection treatment for every S5 PCB/schematic selectable class and parent/child workspace granularity. 2. Active/inactive pane projections and magenta mutation-authority frame are unambiguous. 3. Selection, hover, optional focus member, locked, hidden, related cross-probe, proposal, and diagnostic precedence is complete. 4. Compound/global/net/connected-run visuals are defined. 5. Internal glow/accent geometry, screen-space sizing, reduced-motion/accessibility, and performance retention law replace conflicting generic halo wording. 6. rendering-study.html and schematic-editor.html remain controlling or are updated with same-size reviewed captures. 7. GUI conformance spec/gates cover tokens, overlay retention, zoom invariance, and HUMAN reference review. 8. Final S5 selection-identity decision cites the completed visual contract.
notes: Research integrated in 4d3b61f. RESOLVED 2026-07-12: glow, active/inactive, Global Net, related, focus, lock, proposal/diagnostic, bus hierarchy, and text/point treatment. Text selects glyphs without persistent bbox; junction/via keep semantic/material core + ring/glow; no-connect full X; Symbol Editor pin complete pin; tiny/high-contrast minimum crisp cues. Remaining: dense-selection fallback, full per-class matrix reconciliation and conformance/prototype closure.

title: EPIC: Pro-grade native terminal emulator (Ghostty/Alacritty-class)
description: Build Datum's embedded terminal into a pro-grade native emulator: real cell grid, full color/attributes, Unicode width, scrollback, reflow; run code agents + bash/python in multi-tab sessions; preserve the sanctioned Datum context back-door (env + .datum/ sidecars). Decision: BUILD the state model (Datum is ~60% there via the mature parser+codec), on three guardrails — link unicode-width/unicode-segmentation (don't hand-roll Unicode), gate on vttest+esctest2 conformance suites, decline the graphics ceiling (no sixel/kitty/iTerm2). Architecture is adopt-agnostic: the wgpu render interface + portable-pty + focus authority are identical whether the core is Datum's own Grid<Cell> or alacritty_terminal, so the core stays swappable and the build is reversible. Full detail: research/terminal-redesign/TERMINAL_CURRENT_STATE_MAP.md + TERMINAL_REDESIGN_ARCHITECTURE.md. To be ratified as decision 024 + Active Frontier placement.

title: Phase 0: swap hand-rolled libc PTY for portable-pty
description: Replace the ~90 lines of unsafe libc PTY (posix_openpt/grantpt/unlockpt/ptsname_r + pre_exec setsid/TIOCSCTTY/dup2, ioctl resize) in terminal_session.rs with portable-pty (wezterm's crate): native_pty_system().openpty, CommandBuilder+spawn_command, try_clone_reader (reader thread unchanged), take_writer, master.resize. Retain a thin libc::killpg for PROCESS-GROUP signals (Ctrl-C to child pipelines) — portable-pty's Child::kill hits leader only. Threading model unchanged. Context back-door env injection moves Command->CommandBuilder::env (mechanical). Gains cross-platform (ConPTY) for free.

title: Phase 0: Grid->(Quad,TextRun) render interface + font-measured cell metric
description: Stand up the terminal render interface in gui-render: TerminalGrid::emit(origin,cell_w,cell_h,damage) -> (Vec<Quad> bg+underline/strike+cursor, Vec<TextRun> glyphs). Datum's GPU path is ALREADY two-pass (quads before text); Quad is the bg-rect primitive, TextRun already carries truecolor color:[f32;3]. The single new contract: font-derived (cell_w,cell_h) — measure one IBM Plex Mono glyph advance per size (machinery exists at geometry.rs:1250), wide chars = 2*cell_w + spacer. Replaces the hardcoded 7.9px advance, the .take(180) truncation, and the stringly-typed color table in bottom_dock.rs (deleted). Lock the interface first (even over the current model) so the grid build slots behind it.

title: Phase 1: cell-grid data model + lossless color/attributes
description: Replace TerminalLaneState's Vec<String>+RLE styled_lines with Grid<Cell{grapheme, fg:Rgb, bg:Rgb, flags}>; retarget the (kept) parser's writes (put_char_at/set_style_at) from strings to the grid; delete the RLE span-shuffle machinery (terminal_style.rs coalescing, terminal_grid.rs insert/delete/erase string juggling -> grid row-copy ops). Lossless render: paint bg rects, underline/strike/overline, 256-color+truecolor — everything the parser already captures reaches the screen. Move the grid OUT of gui-protocol into gui-app (it never serializes; mis-filed as scene state); shrink the boundary type to terminal chrome only (title/cwd/bell/tabs/cursor-style/modes).

title: Phase 2: Unicode width + scrollback/alt-screen separation
description: Link unicode-width + unicode-segmentation. Stop using chars().count() for columns; advance cursor by cell width; store a grapheme cluster per lead cell; combining marks width 0, wide chars width 2 (lead+spacer). Separate visible screen / bounded scrollback ring / alt-screen (DEC 1049 leaves scrollback untouched) — removes the MAX_TERMINAL_ROWS=240 conflation. Gate hard on esctest2 + a curated CJK/emoji width corpus — this is the permanent correctness tax (width drifts yearly, is relative to tmux/other terminals).

title: Phase 3: reflow on resize
description: Highest-risk single feature. Record wrapped-vs-hard line continuation in the grid; rewrap logical lines on column change, preserving cursor, wide-cell boundaries, scroll region, and scrollback. Impossible on the string model — depends on the cell grid (Phase 1) and width (Phase 2). Alacritty shipped reflow years in and kept generating bugs; gate on esctest2 + resize corpus.

title: Phase 4: damage tracking + DEC-mode/OSC polish
description: Per-row/per-cell dirty flags so gui-render re-uploads only changed rows (perf at scale). Round out DEC breadth the parser already dispatches: cursor style (DECSCUSR), synchronized output (2026), focus reporting (1004); charset/DEC line-drawing (currently the charset designation is parsed then DISCARDED -> box-drawing TUIs break). Optional OSC 8 hyperlinks / OSC 52 clipboard with security caveats. EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE: sixel, iTerm2, kitty graphics protocols; per-terminal width correction tables.
